reason: Environment is non-functional for this task: (1) Bash tool fails on every invocation with `EACCES: permission denied, mkdir '/home/worker/.claude/session-env'` (even with dangerouslyDisableSandbox=true), so docker build, oracle validation (nop=0/gold=1), and any shell work are blocked. (2) Edit/Write into `/workspace/task/tests/test_outputs.py` fails with `EACCES: permission denied, open '/workspace/task/tests/test_outputs.py'` — the tests/ subdirectory is read-only for this process, while writes to /workspace/task/ root succeed. Both prior sessions (reconcile and tests_rewrite) hit the same blocker and abandoned. Without the ability to edit the test file or run the oracle, the rewrite cannot be performed or validated. A ready-to-apply replacement that addresses solution_uniqueness_guard exists at /workspace/task/_proposed_test_outputs.py; manual application would be: cp /workspace/task/_proposed_test_outputs.py /workspace/task/tests/test_outputs.py. Note that the Tier C `behavior_in_tests` rubric (asks for actual Bun.serve/net.connect smuggling rejection at runtime) is also structurally unfixable in this environment because the Dockerfile uses prebuilt oven/bun:1.2.4-slim — the C++ HttpParser.h source patch is never recompiled into the running bun binary, so a runtime HTTP behavior test could never observe the fix and would fail on both nop and gold.
